mnemonics.title: Dynamic Array Growth
mnemonics.content: GROW DOUBLY: Geometric growth (factor 2) Results in O(n) total cost for n insertions, Only Way to achieve O(1) amortized insert, Doubling prevents quadratic behavior, Using arithmetic growth yields poor performance.

mnemonics.title: Dynamic Array Benefits
mnemonics.content: FAST ACCESS: Fast random Access by index, All elements Contiguous in memory, Stores primitives efficiently, Takes constant O(1) time for end operations.

mnemonics.title: Capacity vs Size
mnemonics.content: CUP SIZE: Capacity is the Pot (total space), Use (size) is what's actually filled, imagine CUP with SIZE markings.

acronyms.usage: Stack property - dynamic arrays excel at implementing LIFO structures
Queue property - dynamic arrays can implement queues with front/rear pointers
Error when dynamic array cannot allocate more memory during resize
Problem when capacity far exceeds size; use shrink-to-fit

visualTips.visualization: Picture a bucket filling up: when it reaches the brim (capacity), you get a bucket twice as big and transfer all water (elements) to the new bucket. Most fills only add water without changing buckets.
Think of houses on a street (contiguous) vs tents scattered in a field (linked). Walking down the street (iteration) is faster than traveling between scattered tents (cache misses).
Like a subway card: occasional expensive rides (resizing) are paid for by many cheap rides (regular inserts). Average cost per ride is low.
A water tank with a float indicator: when the tank is too empty (low load factor), you might downsize; when nearly full, you prepare for more water (resize).

analogies.analogy: Like a hotel that can add floors when full. Most days, new guests just get a room (O(1)). When completely full, they build a new hotel twice the size and move everyone (O(n) but rare). The average guest experiences only O(1) wait time.

Contiguous is like a movie theater with assigned seats - you can go directly to seat 50. Linked is like asking each person for the next person's location - you must ask 1, then 2, then 3 to find person 50.
Like a company laying off workers when projects dry up - they downsize office space to save rent, but not after every single departure, only when significantly under capacity.
